headline: liliana-vess.md four-demon arc paragraph contains 4 distinct canon-falsifiable errors in a single sentence-cluster — wrong contract chronology (Griselbrand/Kothophed swap), wrong Griselbrand killing-context, wrong Razaketh killer, wrong Bolas-binding timing. belzenlok.md repeats the chronology error.
summary: cards/_characters/liliana-vess.md line 62 makes four distinct claims about Liliana Vess's four-demon contract arc, all of which fail verification against Wizards's own canonical story articles + MTG Wiki + Wikipedia. (1) **Contract chronology**: line 62 names the demons 'in order' as 'Griselbrand (killed off-page pre-Innistrad-block, possibly by Liliana herself), Kothophed (killed by Liliana with the Chain Veil during Magic Origins), Razaketh...' The canonical kill order per multiple sources is Kothophed FIRST (Magic Origins, 2015), Griselbrand SECOND (Avacyn Restored, 2012 release order but Origins is the chronological narrative-event source for the kill); ordering Griselbrand before Kothophed reverses the actual chronology. (2) **Griselbrand killing-context**: line 62 says Griselbrand was 'killed off-page pre-Innistrad-block, possibly by Liliana herself' — actually Griselbrand was killed BY Liliana ON-PAGE DURING the Innistrad block (Avacyn Restored, 2012), after the Helvault was destroyed and freed him. Per multiple sources: Liliana 'tracked Griselbrand down, slaughtering both angels and demons alike, and finally cornered him... she saw through his lies and used the Chain Veil to kill him permanently.' Not pre-block, not off-page, not 'possibly by Liliana' (it IS by Liliana, confirmed). (3) **Razaketh killer**: line 62 says Razaketh was 'killed by Nicol Bolas during the Hour of Devastation' — WRONG. Verified via the primary Wizards source (magic.wizards.com — Feast, by Kelly Digges, 2017-06-14 — the canonical Hour of Devastation story chapter for the Razaketh confrontation): **Liliana kills Razaketh**, not Bolas. The article's climax: 'With her consciousness divided among the two dozen dead crocodiles in front of her, she gnashed her teeth and attacked... The crocodiles dragged the remains of the demon into the river of blood.' Razaketh attempts to control Liliana via a hidden contract clause; the Gatewatch interrupts the control; Liliana then uses necromancy to raise dead crocodiles from the Luxa and tear Razaketh apart. (4) **Bolas-binding timing**: line 62 says the Razaketh kill 'free[d] one of Liliana's contracts but bind[ed] her to Bolas in the process' — WRONG timing. Liliana's contract did not default to Bolas during HOU (Razaketh kill in 2017); it defaulted to Bolas at the END of DOM (Belzenlok kill, 2018) per Wizards's Return to Dominaria Episode 12 coda. Bolas reveals himself as her contract-holder only after Belzenlok's death, NOT after Razaketh's. The belzenlok.md node body line 66 also carries the chronology error from (1): 'chronologically following Griselbrand, Kothophed, and Razaketh' reverses the Griselbrand/Kothophed pair.

primary_wizards_source_razaketh.title: Feast
primary_wizards_source_razaketh.verbatim_climax: With her consciousness divided among the two dozen dead crocodiles in front of her, she gnashed her teeth and attacked. Her two dozen bellies hungered, and her two dozen jaws opened wide. The crocodiles dragged the remains of the demon into the river of blood, splashing crimson arcs into the air as their tails violently slapped the surface of the water.

evidence.wikipedia_griselbrand_killing_context: Per Wikipedia + multiple secondary sources: Griselbrand was released from the Helvault during Avacyn Restored, then later killed by Liliana using the Chain Veil during the Innistrad-block storyline conclusion (~2012). The kill is on-page, not off-page, and is unambiguously by Liliana.
evidence.bolas_binding_canonical_timing: Bolas's default-clause activation occurs at end of DOM (Episode 12 coda, 2018-05-30), not during HOU (2017). Per Episode 12: after the Belzenlok kill, 'Liliana looked down at herself, but the lines of her pact were still written on her skin'; Bolas then reveals himself as her new master. The HOU-era binding-to-Bolas claim conflates two distinct narrative beats.

errors: Demon kill chronology lists Griselbrand before Kothophed; canonical order is Kothophed first (Magic Origins), Griselbrand second (Avacyn Restored)

'Griselbrand (killed off-page pre-Innistrad-block, possibly by Liliana herself)' — wrong on three counts: (a) killed DURING Innistrad block not pre-Innistrad, (b) killed ON-PAGE not off-page, (c) killed BY Liliana not 'possibly by Liliana'

'Razaketh (killed by Nicol Bolas during the Hour of Devastation, freeing one of Liliana's contracts but binding her to Bolas in the process)' — wrong on two counts: (a) Razaketh was killed by Liliana, not Bolas (primary source: Feast, Kelly Digges, 2017-06-14), (b) the Bolas-binding happened at end of DOM after the Belzenlok kill, not during HOU after the Razaketh kill

carriers.error: 'chronologically following Griselbrand, Kothophed, and Razaketh' — Griselbrand/Kothophed order reversed; canonical chronology is Kothophed (1st), Griselbrand (2nd), Razaketh (3rd)

fix.approach: 2 surgical edits across 2 files. Rewrite liliana-vess.md line 62's four-demon paragraph with canon-grounded chronology and killing-context for each demon. Fix belzenlok.md line 66's Griselbrand/Kothophed swap. All canonical claims grounded in primary Wizards story sources (Feast 2017-06-14 for Razaketh, Return to Dominaria Episode 12 for Belzenlok and the Bolas-default).

edits.old: The family-and-mentor thread for Liliana is replaced, structurally, by the four-demon contract arc: Liliana's character is defined not by mentor-figures (the Origins flashback gestures at her brother Josu as the precipitating-grief figure, parallel to Gideon's Irregulars) but by the four demons she sold her soul to in exchange for unnaturally extended life and necromantic power. The contracts in order: **Griselbrand** (killed off-page pre-Innistrad-block, possibly by Liliana herself), **Kothophed** (killed by Liliana with the Chain Veil during Magic Origins), **Razaketh** (killed by Nicol Bolas during the Hour of Devastation, freeing one of Liliana's contracts but binding her to Bolas in the process), and finally **Belzenlok** (killed by Liliana with the help of the Weatherlight crew during Dominaria). The Belzenlok kill freed her from three of the four demonic contracts but the Bolas-contract remained; the WAR climax is the moment she broke that last contract by turning the Dreadhorde — Bolas's own army, raised by her necromancy under his command — against him.
edits.new: The family-and-mentor thread for Liliana is replaced, structurally, by the four-demon contract arc: Liliana's character is defined not by mentor-figures (the Origins flashback gestures at her brother Josu as the precipitating-grief figure, parallel to Gideon's Irregulars) but by the four demons she sold her soul to in exchange for unnaturally extended life and necromantic power. The contracts in canonical kill order: **Kothophed** (killed first, by Liliana with the Chain Veil after he sent her to retrieve the Veil from Shandalar; Magic Origins, 2015), **Griselbrand** (killed second, by Liliana with the Chain Veil during the Innistrad block; after the Helvault was destroyed Liliana tracked him down, slaughtered angels and demons to corner him, saw through his bargaining attempts and ended him permanently), **Razaketh** (killed third, by Liliana on Amonkhet during *Hour of Devastation*; Razaketh attempted to seize control of Liliana's body via a hidden contract clause, the Gatewatch interrupted the control just long enough for Liliana to free herself, she then raised dead crocodiles from the Luxa River and tore the demon apart — primary source: Wizards's 'Feast' by Kelly Digges, 2017-06-14), and finally **Belzenlok** (killed fourth, by Liliana with Gideon Jura wielding the Blackblade at the Cabal Stronghold in Urborg during Dominaria, 2018). The Belzenlok kill APPEARED to free her from the four-demon arc but the contract defaulted to Nicol Bolas in the Episode 12 coda — Bolas had brokered the original deal and revealed himself as her new contract-holder when the Gatewatch departed Dominaria. The WAR climax is the moment she broke that final contract by turning the Dreadhorde — Bolas's own army, raised by her necromancy under his command — against him.

edits.old: Belzenlok was the fourth and final demonlord on Liliana Vess's contract after the Mending — chronologically following Griselbrand, Kothophed, and Razaketh.
edits.new: Belzenlok was the fourth and final demonlord on Liliana Vess's contract after the Mending — chronologically following Kothophed (killed first via the Chain Veil during Magic Origins), Griselbrand (killed second by Liliana during the Innistrad block after the Helvault was destroyed), and Razaketh (killed third by Liliana on Amonkhet during Hour of Devastation).

neighbor_spotcheck: Cross-checked all corpus files mentioning 'Razaketh', 'Griselbrand', or 'Kothophed': only liliana-vess.md and belzenlok.md carry the four-demon arc claims. No other character / symbol / hub layer node makes these specific claims; no card-trivia file carries them either. Pattern is isolated to the two character-layer nodes. The belzenlok.md error is identical-shape to liliana-vess.md's error (the same Griselbrand/Kothophed swap), suggesting one node imported chronology from the other during commission — most likely belzenlok.md (newer, wave 212) imported from liliana-vess.md (older). Fixing both together prevents future re-import drift.
isolation_check: Pattern is isolated to one paragraph in each of two layer nodes (1 four-error paragraph in liliana-vess.md + 1 chronology-only error in belzenlok.md). The same anti-pattern family as CBG-009 (m21-dog-tribal Rosewater fabrication) and CBG-019 (angrath Blogatog misattribution) — body-prose lore claims that fail primary-source verification. The compounding novelty here: a single 3-sentence paragraph in liliana-vess.md carries FOUR distinct canon-falsifiable claims, the highest-density Edgelord-rigor-audit catch CBG has surfaced. All four errors share a single root: the four-demon arc was reconstructed from memory/secondary-source-summary without a Wizards-primary-source verification pass at commission time.
pattern_alert: MTG planeswalker character nodes that summarize multi-set arc chronologies (Liliana's four-demon arc, Bolas's invasion arc, Gideon's death arc, Chandra's escape-from-fundamentalists arc) need primary-source verification of every chronological claim before merging. The Wikipedia + MTG Wiki summaries are useful but error-prone at the chronology level — secondary sources frequently conflate adjacent arc beats (Razaketh-control by Bolas vs Razaketh-kill by Liliana, Bolas-binding via HOU contract vs Bolas-binding via DOM default clause). When commissioning a planeswalker character node, the discipline should be: WebFetch the actual Magic Story chapter for each named kill / arc beat, capture verbatim quotes for the load-bearing claims, and refuse to import unverified chronology from MTG Wiki summary paragraphs.
